acceptance_targets: [CH12_CRAFT_SANITIZED_SPENDING_MONTAGE] Pass if the revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:178-190 contains at least two discrete on-page decision beats in which a need is presented, Kem actively chooses to spend, the ability use is rendered physically, and Vel or Kem registers the cost immediately afterward. Fail if the section still reads primarily as a catalogue of interventions with level totals.

[CH12_DIALOGUE_FALLOW_CALIBRATION_LINE_REUSE] Pass if those three spans still convey the same information but no clause from the current lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:224, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:246, or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:272 survives verbatim except unavoidable world terms, and each rewritten span includes at least one concrete local detail not present in the current phrasing. Fail if the exchange still contains the current 'hundred and forty,' 'I've seen it twice,' or 'zero on purpose' formulations.

[CH12_LEVEL_ARITHMETIC] All level transitions in Ch12 are arithmetically consistent. Each named spend decrements by the stated cost, and the running total matches at every checkpoint. No downstream chapters reference a level that contradicts the corrected count.
[R03] The girl at L2897 does not re-inventory Kem's boots, hands, and Vault markers in the same sequence as the woman at L2814.

[LW05-002] When chapters 09-12 are read straight through, at least one of the 09/10, 10/11, or 11/12 handoffs now carries immediate emotional or logistical fallout on-page instead of opening with another elapsed-days reorientation.

[LW05-003] By the end of chapter 12, the Vel/Kem partnership occupies a materially different state than it did at the end of chapters 10 and 11: explicit terms, explicit breach, or explicit recommitment rather than another silent continuation.
[length_chapter_12_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 5500 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:178-190 contains at least two discrete on-page decision beats in which a need is presented, Kem actively chooses to spend, the ability use is rendered physically, and Vel or Kem registers the cost immediately afterward. Fail if the section still reads primarily as a catalogue of interventions with level totals.
findings.problem: The chapter's core dramatic engine is Kem burning through the reserve Vel needs for the Scour, but once that conflict peaks the draft retreats into montage. The fifteen-level irrigation repair and the final twenty levels of smaller interventions are mostly delivered as summarized inventory with costs attached, so the chapter stops dramatizing conscience as repeated choice and starts accounting for it. In this book's terms that is sanitization: scene pressure and bodily consequence are replaced by report language at the exact point the chapter most needs presence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the post-wall descent at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:178-190 so at least one infrastructure repair beat and one person-to-person healing beat are fully scened rather than listed. Keep the arithmetic, but make the request, Kem's decision, the felt ability-use, and the recipient's immediate reaction occur on-page. If needed, sharpen Vel's budget pressure earlier at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:166-176 so the later spends read as chosen costs instead of a recap.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if those three spans still convey the same information but no clause from the current lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:224, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:246, or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:272 survives verbatim except unavoidable world terms, and each rewritten span includes at least one concrete local detail not present in the current phrasing. Fail if the exchange still contains the current 'hundred and forty,' 'I've seen it twice,' or 'zero on purpose' formulations.
findings.problem: Fallow's chapter-defining exchange is reusing calibration-only example-line material from the style bible: the sent-out-at-one-forty phrasing, the 'I've seen it twice' vanishing formulation, and the 'zero on purpose' beat all arrive near-verbatim. That is explicitly disallowed, and because the borrowing happens in the scene that identifies the Scour, it makes Fallow sound prewritten from notes instead of locally alive on the page.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite Fallow's lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:224-225, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:242-250, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:268-276 from scratch. Preserve the facts that Fallow started high, has witnessed two vanishings, and recognizes Kem's intent, but replace the current sentence shapes and punchline phrasing with fresh, exhausted, scene-specific diction grounded in what Fallow saw and what this conversation costs them to say.

findings.acceptance_test: All level transitions in Ch12 are arithmetically consistent. Each named spend decrements by the stated cost, and the running total matches at every checkpoint. No downstream chapters reference a level that contradicts the corrected count.
findings.evidence: Line 2995: Vel's Perception sweep is narrated as taking her from 'Nineteen to eighteen.' Line 3089: after a bridge reinforcement that costs one additional level, the text reads 'Eighteen now.' Two one-level spends from 19 should leave Vel at 17, not 18.
findings.problem: The novel's level-tracking system is a core narrative mechanic and a primary source of stakes. Every spend is meant to be felt. An arithmetic error in the count undermines the system's credibility at exactly the point where the reader should trust it most — the Margins chapters, where every level matters more because fewer remain. An attentive reader (and award jurors are attentive readers) will catch this and question whether other counts are reliable.
findings.rewrite_direction: Correct the arithmetic. After the Perception sweep (19 → 18) and the bridge reinforcement (18 → 17), the text at line 3089 should read 'Seventeen now' or equivalent. Verify downstream level references in Ch12–13 are consistent with the corrected count. If the downstream math depends on Vel being at 18, adjust the Perception sweep cost instead (e.g., make it a zero-cost passive read rather than an active spend).

findings.acceptance_test: The girl at L2897 does not re-inventory Kem's boots, hands, and Vault markers in the same sequence as the woman at L2814.
findings.evidence: Ch12 L2814: 'Her eyes went to Kem first, to his height, his hands, the boots that were battered now but still identifiably Vault-made'; Ch12 L2897: 'The girl looked at Kem's boots, his hands, the remains of Vault that he still carried.'
findings.problem: Two outsider characters assess Kem's appearance within 83 lines of each other in the same chapter, both cataloging the same details (boots, hands, Vault residue) in the same order with the same framing (stranger-assessment). The reader receives an identical characterization beat twice.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep L2814 (the first outsider assessment, which introduces the Margins settlement). Rewrite L2897 so the girl's attention lands on something the first assessment did not cover, or remove the physical-inventory framing entirely and let the girl respond to Kem's behavior or speech instead.

findings.acceptance_test: When chapters 09-12 are read straight through, at least one of the 09/10, 10/11, or 11/12 handoffs now carries immediate emotional or logistical fallout on-page instead of opening with another elapsed-days reorientation.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2143; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2148; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2478; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2483; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2799; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2808

evidence.excerpt: 2143: Sleep came in pieces. In the last one before dawn, his father sat in the Conservatory with the usual blank eyes and a Ledger at zero. This time the face was not empty. Kem woke before the dream could make sense of that, to gray light, white ash, and Vel still sitting upright where he'd left her, watching the trees.

2148: Four days after the grain-silo fight, the settlement looked the same. Dren's left arm had spent those four days swelling around Kem's ugly stitches, Vel's ribs still bit every time the path dropped hard under her boots, and that morning she'd hauled them west off the south road to come here.
2478: The hills lifted toward the river. Heat shook above the ground. Vel adjusted her pack and followed them south.

2483: Three days after they left Thessa's settlement, they found the bridge. Kem's shoulders still remembered every borrowed bit of Strength he'd used to drag the pack up bad hills, and Thessa's question had only gotten meaner with distance. What remained of the bridge, gray timbers slumped into the current and iron bolts orange with rust, suggested years of neglect rather than sudden collapse. The southern end had rotted away entirely. Northern pilings stood like teeth in a broken jaw, with water rushing between them in brown surges that carried sticks and clods of soil.

2799: She made no entry in the field notes. The observation log she had been maintaining out of bureaucratic reflex stayed blank. Instead she sat with the fire she had built, the calluses on her hands, and the number 270 that was hers, and let the question remain where it was, uncategorized, unprocessed, without protocol for what to do next.

2808: They'd crossed the river two days ago. Since then the land had changed in stages, first the forest thinning, then the soil lightening, then the structures getting worse. Now there was no pretending they were anywhere but the Margins.

findings.problem: Three consecutive openings convert charged chapter endings into off-page elapsed time. Chapter 10 jumps four days after the threshold reveal, chapter 11 jumps three days after Kem's breach with Vel at Thessa's settlement, and chapter 12 jumps two days past the river lesson. The repeated reset rhythm makes the window feel like a string of set-pieces rather than a compounding run.
findings.rewrite_direction: Break the pattern at a boundary, preferably in chapter 11 or chapter 12. Let one opening begin inside the unresolved aftermath of the prior ending before widening to the next location, so the reader experiences the carryover instead of being told time has passed.

findings.acceptance_test: By the end of chapter 12, the Vel/Kem partnership occupies a materially different state than it did at the end of chapters 10 and 11: explicit terms, explicit breach, or explicit recommitment rather than another silent continuation.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2354; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2477; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2671; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2971; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3121; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3123

evidence.excerpt: 2354: "I knew I couldn't cross alone," she said.

2671: Five days since Thessa's kitchen, and the question still sat where it had landed. Was Vel teaching him to judge for himself, or building the kind of man she needed? Both possibilities fit too neatly to let the matter rest.

2971: She needed to say it plain: *every level you spend on these people is a level we need at the Scour.* She needed to explain that the cascade mechanism wasn't elastic, that the threshold required a minimum sustained descent, that his charity was consuming the only resource that stood between them and failure.
3121: Tell him to stop, then. Tell him the rest belongs to the Scour.
3123: Vel kept walking. The words stayed where they'd stuck by the broken wall.

findings.problem: Across chapters 10-12, the Vel/Kem relationship keeps landing in the same leverage shape: Kem learns or senses that Vel has been steering him toward a Scour-related need, Vel only partially voices the truth, and the chapter closes with the two still walking south under damaged trust. The external triggers change, but the emotional turn keeps re-holding instead of moving to a distinct new state.
findings.rewrite_direction: Make chapter 12 pay off the accumulated pressure instead of replaying it. Either Vel states the budget truth plainly, Kem names the manipulation himself, or the pair explicitly renegotiates the descent on new terms. Do not end on a third version of Vel swallowing the line.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 5500 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

Every narration sentence must be grammatically complete. Do not use article-stripped noun-phrase fragments, subjectless stubs, or telegraphic fragment strings as a rhythmic device. Isolated level-count callouts ('Twelve.' or 'Zero.') are acceptable; stacked fragment clusters ('Not night. Underground dark.' or 'Too slow. Too loud. Too late.') are not. Achieve compression through short complete sentences, not by dropping subjects and articles.

When a scene beat has landed through concrete image, action, dialogue, or bodily sensation, do not add an abstract summary sentence that restates its meaning in thesis language. If a paragraph's only job is to translate what the reader already understood from the prior image or line of dialogue, cut it or replace it with a new fact, consequence, or pressure. Trust the scene. One beat, one landing.

No character should deliver a multi-sentence thematic diagnosis, balanced mini-speech, or quotable aphorism during a pressured scene. Accusations, confessions, and revelations must arrive through messy, character-true speech: interruptions, self-corrections, false starts, incomplete grammar, practical redirects, and mid-sentence recalibrations. If a line of dialogue can be lifted as a standalone epigram, it is probably too composed for the scene pressure. Rhetorical questions used for emphasis rather than genuine inquiry are not permitted for Vel.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This is a dark comedy about a world where doing nothing is rational and doing something is self-destruction. Both the comedy and the darkness must be fully committed. Do not soften combat into choreography or spectacle — fights should hurt, cost real things, and leave damage. Do not soften the comedy into gentle satire — the absurdity of the Hoarded and their agents should be genuinely funny, sometimes cruelly so. Do not soften grief into sentimentality or revelation into epiphany. The premise demands a tonal register that holds absurdity and genuine pain in the same hand. When the material is uncomfortable — the physical reality of injury, the moral weight of exploitation, the specific grief of losing someone to a place you cannot reach — lean into specificity rather than retreating to safety. A draft that never risks anything produces a novel that never moves anyone.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in combat, injury, consequence, or emotional extremity is a craft failure. If Vel breaks ribs, the reader should feel the break — the specific wrongness of breathing with cracked bone, the way she holds herself, the pain when she forgets. If Kem is terrified, the terror should be specific and physical: hands, stomach, breathing, the precise quality of his fear. Do not abstract away from embodied experience into summary or generalization.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Sable (low) and early-book Kem (initially low, shifting to high). Uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, formal Hoarded speech contexts, or bureaucratic register. In casual conversation, unjustified full-form lines should be treated as suspect.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Vary chapter rhythm aggressively. Combat chapters should be compressed and physical. Discovery chapters should have more room for observation and the surprise of new information. Quiet chapters should breathe — longer scenes, more sensory detail, conversations that circle rather than drive. Intercut chapters (like Ch 11's Kem/Sable split and Ch 17's Sable intercut) should have contrasting textures between sections. No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural shape: vary scene count, dialogue density, pacing, and the ratio of action to interiority. Dark comedy chapters in the back half (Ch 12, 14, 15, 17) must commit fully to comic energy as a primary narrative mode: the absurdity of Hoarded bureaucratic language applied to human exploitation, the grim humor of fighting for your life with single-digit levels, the comedy of precision teamwork performed in emotional silence. These chapters carry genuine stakes alongside their comedy — the tonal fusion is the point, not tonal alternation.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, physical, occasionally wry. Avoids both literary pretension and genre cliche. The world has its own vocabulary (Hoarded, Spent, Ledger, Stillness, the Scour, the Margins) and these terms should be used naturally, not defined in exposition. Metaphors earned from the world's own materials — spending, counting, weight, erosion — rather than imported from outside the setting. The Hoarded institutional register — bureaucratic classifications, procedural euphemism, administrative language applied to human exploitation — is a specific comic instrument in the Margins chapters. When rendering Hoarded documents, policies, or institutional speech, the language should be precise, procedural, and devastating in its indifference.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Narrator-as-comedian: comedy arises from situation and character, not from narrative commentary or winking asides to the reader
Ability-use described like video game tooltips: do not produce stat readouts or system notifications in prose
Emotional temperature permanently at maximum: not every scene is a crisis and not every internal moment is an epiphany
Generic fantasy description: landscape, architecture, and weather must be specific to this world's conditions, not recycled from the fantasy-prose commons
Competence porn: do not let characters be consistently impressive — they should fumble, miscalculate, and fail at things that are hard
Thematic underlining: the prose should not highlight its own meanings or signal when something is symbolically significant
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third-person limited, shifting with the POV character. Vel chapters are compressed and physical — the prose should feel like it is counting. Kem chapters are more expansive and curious — the prose has more room for observation and surprise. Sable chapters are structured and institutional, loosening as she changes. The POV break in Ch 13–14 (two consecutive Kem chapters) should make Vel's absence felt: the reader is locked inside Kem's betrayed perspective, and the prose should reflect the claustrophobia of a mind cycling through anger without access to the other person's interiority. The distance should be intimate enough to access characters' internal arithmetic but not so close it becomes stream-of-consciousness.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by chapter texture and scene pressure. Short declarative sentences in combat. Longer, conditional, clause-heavy sentences in reflection. Comma-heavy run-ons in panic or exhilaration. The Ledger's arithmetic should bleed into prose rhythm during ability-use moments — characters counting, weighing, calculating. Quiet chapters should breathe. Action chapters should compress. Two consecutive chapters should not share the same dominant rhythm.
sensory_bias: proprioceptive awareness — the body's internal sense of position, strain, damage, and capacity
physical weight and effort — characters feel gravity, distance, fatigue
the Ledger's feedback — a character's awareness of their remaining levels as a persistent background sensation
environmental specificity — terrain, weather, architecture rendered through what a body encounters
